reason: Authoritative architecture baseline for scanner-first MCP, scoring, config, risk, storage, chart, webhook, and non-execution boundaries.
Backend spec entry point for applicable implementation guidelines.
Current Kairos module layout and architecture drift notes.
Project quality rules, type annotations, testing expectations, and no fake market data constraint.
Error propagation and graceful degradation expectations for partial scanner failures.
Storage section is part of the first-phase config surface even though persistence is mostly out of scope.
Logging expectations for scanner warnings and failures.
Cross-layer and code-reuse thinking applies to MCP schema/config/scanner changes.
Prior recorded research supporting schema stability, retries, and signal-vs-execution boundaries.
